volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Persian (Section 3.1.342 - Section 3.3.439b)

sections.label: Section 3.1.342
sections.original: Vtrum hercle magis in ventris rem videbitur .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , that of the two which shall appear most for the interest of my stomach ; it ’ s my authority over you , I suppose , not yours over me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.344
sections.original: Tua istaéc potestas est , pater . verum tamen ,
sections.translation: This power is yours , father ; but still ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.345
sections.original: quamquam res nostrae sunt , pater , pauperculae ,
sections.translation: although our circumstances are but very limited , it ’ s better to pass our lives with frugality and moderation ; for if disgrace is added to poverty , poverty will be more unendurable , our character more frail .

sections.label: Section 3.1.349
sections.translation: Why really you are impertinent .

sections.label: Section 3.1.349b
sections.original: Non sum , neque me esse arbitror ,
sections.translation: I am not , nor do I think that I am ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.350
sections.original: quom parva natu recte praecipio patri .
sections.translation: when , though of youthful age , I give good advice to my father . For enemies carry about slander not in the form in which it took its rise .

sections.label: Section 3.1.352
sections.translation: Let them carry it about , and let them go to utter and extreme perdition . I don ’ t value all their enmities any more than if an empty table were now set before me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.355
sections.translation: Father , the scandal of men is everlasting ; even then does it survive , when you would suppose it to be dead .

sections.label: Section 3.1.357
sections.translation: What ? Are you afraid lest I should sell you ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.357b
sections.translation: I am not afraid of that , father ; but I wish you not to pretend to do so .

sections.label: Section 3.1.358b
sections.translation: Then it ’ s in vain you wish me not ; this shall be done rather after my own fashion than yours .

sections.label: Section 3.1.360
sections.translation: What is the matter , now ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.360b
sections.translation: Father , reflect upon these words : if a master has threatened punishment to a slave , although it is not intended to be , still , when the whip is taken up , while he is taking off his tunics , with what an amount of misery is he afflicted . Now , that which is not to be , I ’ m still in fear of .

sections.label: Section 3.1.365
sections.original: Virgo atque mulier nulla erit quin sit mala ,
sections.translation: Damsel or woman none will there ever be , but what she must be good for nothing , who is too wise to be giving satisfaction to her parents .

sections.label: Section 3.1.367
sections.original: Virgo atque mulier nulla erit quin sit mala ,
sections.translation: Damsel and woman none can there be , but what she must be good for nothing , who holds her peace if she sees anything going on wrong .

sections.label: Section 3.1.369
sections.translation: ’ Twere better for you to beware of a mischief .

sections.label: Section 3.1.369b
sections.translation: But if I cannot

sections.label: Section 3.1.370
sections.original: cavere , quid agam ? nám ego tibi cautum volo .
sections.translation: beware , what am I to do ? For it ’ s as to yourself I wish to beware .

sections.label: Section 3.1.371
sections.translation: What , am I a mischief ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.371b
sections.original: Non es , neque me dignumst dicere ,
sections.translation: You are not , nor is it becoming for me to say so ; but for this purpose am I using my endeavours , that others may not say so who have that liberty .

sections.label: Section 3.1.373
sections.original: Dicat quod quisque volt ; ego de hac sententia
sections.translation: Let each one say what he pleases ; from this purpose I shall not be moved .

sections.label: Section 3.1.374b
sections.translation: But , could it be after my own way ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.375
sections.translation: you would be acting prudently , rather than foolishly .

sections.label: Section 3.1.375b
sections.translation: It is my pleasure .

sections.label: Section 3.1.376
sections.original: Lubere per me tibi licere intellego ;
sections.translation: I know that I must let it be your pleasure so far as I ’ m concerned ; but it should not please you to be your pleasure , if I had my way .

sections.label: Section 3.1.378
sections.original: Futura es dicto oboediens an non patri ?
sections.translation: Are you going to be obedient to your father ’ s orders , or not ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.379
sections.translation: To be obedient .

sections.label: Section 3.1.379b
sections.translation: Do you know then what I instructed you ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.379c
sections.translation: Everything .

sections.label: Section 3.1.380
sections.translation: Both this , how you were stolen ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.380b
sections.translation: I understand it perfectly well .

sections.label: Section 3.1.381
sections.translation: And who your parents were ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.381b
sections.translation: I keep it in my memory . You cause me of necessity to be artful ; but take you care , when you wish to give me in marriage , that this story doesn ’ t cause the match to be given up .

sections.label: Section 3.1.385
sections.original: Tace , stúlta . non tu nunc hominum mores vides ?
sections.translation: Hold your tongue , simpleton . Do you not see the customs of people now-a-days , that marriage is easily effected here with a reputation of any kind ? So long as there ’ s a marriage-portion , no fault is reckoned as a fault .

sections.label: Section 3.1.388
sections.original: Ergo istuc facito ut veniat in mentem tibi ,
sections.translation: Then take you care , and let this occur to your thoughts , that I am without a fortune .

sections.label: Section 3.1.389b
sections.translation: Take you care , please , how you say that .

sections.label: Section 3.1.390
sections.original: pol deum virtute dicam et maiorum meum ,
sections.translation: By my faith , through the merits of the Gods and of my ancestors I ’ ll say it , you must not say that you are without a fortune , who have a marriage-portion at home . Why look , I ’ ve got a whole carriage-full [*] of books at home . If you carefully give your attention to this matter in which we are exerting ourselves , six hundred bon-mots shall be given you out of them as a fortune ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.395
sections.original: atque Attici omnes ; nullum Siculum acceperis :
sections.translation: all Attic ones [*] , too ; you shall not receive a single Sicilian one . With this for a fortune , you might safely marry a beggar even [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.1.397
sections.original: Quin tu me ducis , si quo ducturu'sducturus es , pater ?
sections.translation: Why , then , don ’ t you take me , father , if you are going to take me anywhere ? Either do you sell me , or do with me what you please .

sections.label: Section 3.1.399
sections.translation: You ask what ’ s fair and right . Follow me this way .

sections.label: Section 3.1.399b
sections.translation: I ’ m obedient to your command .

sections.label: Section 3.2.400
sections.original: Quidnam esse acturum hunc dicam vicinum meum ,
sections.translation: I wonder what I ’ m to say my neighbour is going to do , who swore to me that he would pay the money to-day ? But if he should not pay it , and this day go by , I shall have forfeited the money , he his oath . But the door there makes a noise . I wonder who ’ s coming out of doors ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.405
sections.original: Curate isti intus , iam égo domum me recipiam .
sections.translation: Take you care of that in-doors ; I shall betake myself home just now .

sections.label: Section 3.3.405b
sections.translation: Toxilus , how are you ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.406b
sections.translation: How now !— pimping filth mixed up with mud ! How now !— public dung-heap ! dirty , dishonest , lawless , enticer , disgrace to the public ; you hawk after money , greedy and envious ;

sections.label: Section 3.3.410
sections.translation: you impudent , rapacious , craving fellow ( in three hundred lines no person could run through your villanies ), will you take the money ? ( Holding out the purse .) Take the money , will you , shameless fellow . Take hold of the money , will you . Are you going to take the money , then ? Can I make you take the money , filth ? ( Keeps moving it away .)

sections.label: Section 3.3.415
sections.original: non mihi censebas copiam argenti fore ,
sections.translation: You didn ’ t suppose that I should have had so much money — you , who didn ’ t venture to trust me at all except upon oath ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.417
sections.original: Sine respirare me , ut tibi respondeam .
sections.translation: Do let me recover breath , so as to give you an answer . Fellow , dregs of the populace , you stable for she-slaves , you liberator of harlots , you surface for the lash ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.420
sections.translation: you wearer-out of the fetters , you citizen of the treadmill , you slave everlastingly , you gormandizer , glutton , pilferer , runaway , give me the money , will you . Give me the money , impudence . Can I get the money out of you ? Give me the money , I say . Why don ’ t you give me the money ? Are you ashamed of nothing ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.425
sections.original: leno te argentum poscit , solida servitus ,
sections.translation: You impersonation of slavery , a Procurer is asking money of you for the liberation of your mistress , so that all may hear it .

sections.label: Section 3.3.427
sections.original: Tace , obsecro hercle . ne tua vox valide valet .
sections.translation: Troth now , prithee , do hold your tongue . For sure your voice is in first-rate strength .

sections.label: Section 3.3.428
sections.original: Referundae ego habeo linguam natam gratiae .
sections.translation: I ’ ve got a tongue made for returning a compliment . Salt is provided for me at the same price as for yourself .

sections.label: Section 3.3.430
sections.original: nisi me haec defendet , numquam delinget salem .
sections.translation: Unless this tongue protects me , it shall never lick a bit of salt .

sections.label: Section 3.3.431
sections.original: Iam omitte iratus esse . id tibi suscensui ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll cease to be angry now . It was for this I blamed you , because you refused to trust me for the money .

sections.label: Section 3.3.433
sections.original: Mirum quin tibi ego crederém , ut idem mihi
sections.translation: ’ Twas a wonder , indeed , that I didn ’ t trust you , that you might do the same to me that some of the bankers do [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.3.435
sections.original: ubi quid credideris , citius extemplo a foro
sections.translation: When you ’ ve entrusted them with anything , they immediately run more quickly away from the Forum than a hare , when , at the games [*] , he ’ s let out of the entrance of his cage .

sections.label: Section 3.3.437
sections.translation: Take this , will you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.437b
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you give it then ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.437c
sections.translation: There will be here six hundred didrachms , full weight and counted ; cause the damsel to be set at liberty , and bring her out here forthwith .

sections.label: Section 3.3.439b
sections.translation: I ’ ll have her here this moment .
